rulings.Case Title: CORNELIUS MURPHY et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, August 6, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendants Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc. and Kaiser Gypsum Co., Inc.¿s motion to stay is granted with the following conditions [note the language edited by the court]: (1) The matter be stayed pending the ruling of the October 9, 2012 date set for hearing of defendants¿ motion to disqualify plaintiffs¿ counsel per defendants¿ request, and without prejudice to plaintiffs¿ right to oppose any further request for stay; (2) That plaintiffs¿ non-opposition to the requested stay not be deemed in any fashion an admission of the truth as to any purported fact or a concession of the correctness of any legal argument set forth in defendants¿ moving papers in connection to this motion; and (3) That plaintiffs¿ non-opposition to the requested stay shall not be deemed to concede that defendants¿ underlying motion to disqualify has any merit. The court will sign the proposed order already submitted by plaintiffs¿ counsel, with the court¿s edits, at the hearing, which will be at 11:15am at trial call, not at 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. = (503/TLJ)
